match_fields.target_node: National Surgical Adjuvant Breast and Bowel Project

upgrades.notes_append: [Phase2 upgraded institutional_archive -> PMID: 34991349 on 2026-03-18] [Verified 2026-03-18 | Surg Oncol Clin N Am 2022 | content_match] Wickerham DL. 'A Short History of Bernard Fisher's Contributions to Randomized Clinical Trials.' Surg Oncol Clin N Am. 2022 Apr;31(2):141-151. Documents Fisher as founding member (1957-1958) and chairman (1967-1994) of the NSABP.

[Phase2 upgraded institutional_archive -> PMID: 8686806 on 2026-03-18] [Verified 2026-03-18 | Am J Surg 1996 | content_match] Nichols RL. 'Surgical infections: prevention and treatment--1965 to 1995.' Am J Surg. 1996 Jan;171(1):68-74. States 'The Surgical Infection Society (SIS) was established in 1980 for surgeons and other physicians and scientists.' Altemeier documented as founder and first president by SIS institutional sources (Altemeier Lectureship page).

old_values.evidence_locator: University of Cincinnati Department of Surgery Archives

upgrades.notes_append: [Phase2 upgraded institutional_archive -> PMID: 12984268 on 2026-03-18] [VERIFY_NAMES: confirm Altemeier appears as Reid trainee in Carter's enumeration or derivatives] Carter BN. 'The fruition of Halsted's concept of surgical training.' Surgery. 1952. Carter documents the Halsted->Heuer->Reid lineage at Cincinnati. Altemeier's training under Reid (1933-1940) at Cincinnati is confirmed by UC Archives (Altemeier Collection) and SIS institutional records. Alternative: PMID 10551765 (Kerr & O'Leary, Am Surg 1999) discusses the Halstedian training tree.

verification_notes: Edge 3 (Reid -> Altemeier, PMID 12984268) requires verification that Altemeier is mentioned in Carter's enumeration of the Halsted training tree, specifically through the Heuer->Reid->Cincinnati lineage. Altemeier trained under Reid at Cincinnati (1933-1940), not directly under Halsted. Carter's article documents the Cincinnati program as a Halstedian derivative. If Altemeier is not specifically named, the PMID may still be appropriate as it documents the training structure he entered, but consider retaining institutional_archive if the connection is too indirect.
